sources.name: TASVideos — NES / Castlevania 3 / Stair Dismounting Guide
sources.notes: Stair mechanics: pushing up/down = 8 pixels movement, game checks proximity to floor. Grant variable jump confirmed. Character-specific technique documentation.

notes: Castlevania III uses the SAME BASE PHYSICS ENGINE as Castlevania 1 for Trevor Belmont (the main character).
Trevor: walk 1 px/f, fixed jump arc (lookup table), no air control — identical to CV1 Simon.
The major innovation: THREE ALTERNATE PLAYABLE CHARACTERS with completely different mechanics.
Grant DaCnair: wall cling, faster movement, VARIABLE jump height (unusual for CV — contradicts the CV1 fixed arc).
Sypha Belnades: slower movement, magic-based attacks (freeze, fire, lightning).
Alucard: bat form (flight), higher jump, can transform mid-air.
Multi-route game: different stage paths based on which character you rescue.
Stair mechanics confirmed: 8 pixels per input while on staircase.

trevor_belmont._note: Same physics as CV1 Simon Belmont. See castlevania-nes.json for full documentation.

whip.notes: Trevor's whip fires horizontally only — same as CV1. NOT 8-directional (that's SCV4).

trevor_belmont.cross_reference: See castlevania-nes.json for complete physics documentation of Trevor's mechanics.

grant_dacnair._note: Most mobile character — wall cling and variable jump make him the preferred speedrun character for most routes.
grant_dacnair.notes: Grant is faster than Trevor. Exact px/frame value needs ROM verification.

notes: Grant has VARIABLE JUMP HEIGHT — unique among CV1/CV3 characters.
This makes Grant feel closer to an action-platformer protagonist than the lookup-table CV1 feel.
TASVideos confirms: 'Grant has variable jump height, which is exploited in various techniques.'

wall_cling.description: Grant can cling to walls and ceiling by pressing toward them while airborne.
notes: From wall/ceiling: can launch off in any direction.
One frame release while walking off ledge causes instant maximum fall speed (speed preservation technique).
Wall cling enables traversal of vertical shafts not accessible to Trevor.

weapon.notes: Grant throws knives that can be rapidly thrown by canceling the throw animation (frame-precise technique).

sypha_belnades._note: Slowest character but with powerful magic capable of crowd control.

jump.notes: Sypha's jump is similar to Trevor's — fixed arc. No air control.

freeze.description: Freezes all enemies on screen. Frozen enemies can be used as platforms.
notes: Freeze spell is exploitable: frozen enemy can be stood on, including mid-air enemies.
TASVideos: 'Sypha's freeze spell can be used to disalign yourself from stairs vertically and horizontally.'
Standing on a frozen airborne enemy gives access to otherwise unreachable staircase entrances.

fire.description: Fireballs that travel forward and deal area damage.

alucard._note: Dracula's dhampir son. Highest jump, bat transformation for flight.

jump.notes: Alucard jumps higher than Trevor and has some variable height. Exact values need verification.

bat_form.description: Alucard can transform into a bat for brief flight segments.
notes: Bat form allows sustained flight — different from jumps.
TASVideos: 'Bat Form can be used strategically by switching back to Trevor while partially intersecting with the ground to start a few pixels into the ground.'
Bat form has a duration/energy limitation.
Flight moves at a fixed speed in the direction held.

fireball.description: Alucard's subweapon fires a fireball projectile.

character_switching.description: At certain junction points in the game, Trevor can choose to pair with one of the three alternate characters. Characters are found and rescued during the game.
character_switching.mechanic: Press Select to switch between Trevor and the current partner.
notes: Only one partner can be active at a time.
Switching costs no time but may waste the partner's subweapon.
Different paths unlock different characters: Grant on the graveyard path, Sypha in the forest, Alucard at the Clock Tower.

movement.notes: Each up/down input on a staircase moves character 8 pixels vertically. Same mechanism as CV1.

dismounting.description: Game checks proximity to floor — if standing near a floor edge while on stairs, character dismounts.
dismounting.notes: This dismounting check can be exploited for position manipulation.

subweapons.holy_water: Drops a splash of holy water that burns on the floor
subweapons.axe: Arcing projectile that travels in a high arc
subweapons.notes: Same subweapon system as CV1. Powered by hearts.

invincibility_frames.notes: Same as CV1 (96 frames). VERIFY for CV3.

design_notes_for_giants_drink: CV3's character switching system is a brilliant solution to the 'one-character-one-movement-vocabulary' limitation of CV1. By making the world traversable by four different means, the same geometry has four different difficulty levels and strategies.

Grant's variable jump + wall cling makes him feel like a completely different game within the same level. CV3 levels work with both Trevor's rigid fixed-arc jumps and Grant's flexible variable jumps — a design constraint that forced Konami to think about every gap as solvable multiple ways.

Sypha's freeze spell as a platform generator is a puzzle mechanic: 'freeze the flying enemy, use it as a platform, reach the staircase.' This converts combat tools into traversal tools — elegant design.
The multi-route structure (rescuing different characters unlocks different stage paths) creates meaningful replay value. Each route is a substantially different experience, not just cosmetically.
The character-switching mechanic (Select button) allows real-time tactical adaptation. Expert players learn to switch characters for specific sub-sections within a stage.
